reasoning: The Lean signature faithfully encodes the informal claim. The ambient space `EuclideanSpace ℝ (Fin k)` is the standard formalization of $\mathbb{R}^k$; `¬ Set.Countable E` is "E is uncountable"; the defining predicate `∀ U ∈ 𝓝 x, ¬ Set.Countable (U ∩ E)` is exactly the standard definition of a condensation point; and `Perfect P` in Mathlib is defined as closed with no isolated points, which is the standard notion of a perfect set. Binding both E and P as parameters with the hypothesis `hP` pinning P to the condensation set is equivalent to "let P be the set of condensation points of E". No hypotheses are silently added or dropped.
